name: NumPyro (Probabilistic Programming)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); Uber/Pyro PPL team on JAX

performance.notes: JAX JIT + vectorized MCMC; 100x faster than PyMC for parallel chains via vmap

agent_readiness.notes: Probabilistic programming library built on JAX for high-performance Bayesian inference. NUTS (No-U-Turn Sampler), SVI (Stochastic Variational Inference). Compatible with Pyro's model API. Vectorized parallel MCMC chains via JAX vmap. GPU/TPU acceleration for MCMC. Used for Bayesian deep learning, hierarchical models, causal inference. Significantly faster than PyMC for parallel chains. ArviZ integration for diagnostics.

gotchas: JAX's no-mutation requirement complicates some models
best_when: High-performance Bayesian inference needing GPU/TPU acceleration
When PyMC is too slow for your use case
avoid_when: Beginners to Bayesian inference (PyMC has better learning resources)
When PyMC's mature ecosystem is preferred
summary: NumPyro is a JAX-based probabilistic programming library for high-performance Bayesian inference. NUTS, SVI, vectorized parallel MCMC via JAX vmap. GPU/TPU acceleration. Compatible with Pyro model API. 100x faster than PyMC for parallel chains. ArviZ integration. Best for large-scale Bayesian inference and Bayesian deep learning. Higher learning curve than PyMC — requires JAX familiarity.
